args.query: site:labcorp.com patient billing Medicare bill contact Labcorp

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Billing & Insurance Information | Labcorp

  - url: https://www.labcorp.com/patients/billing

  - source: Labcorp

  - domain: labcorp.com


  ## Content


  # Billing & Insurance Information


  Labcorp provides billing and insurance resources for patients whose laboratory testing
  was performed by Labcorp.


  Labcorp states that it files claims for insured patients directly with Medicare,
  Medicaid, and many insurance companies and managed care plans. Patients should verify
  that the insurance information associated with their testing is current.


  A Labcorp Patient account can be used to review bills and insurance information.
  Insurance information for an invoice can also be updated using the invoice number
  and the requested patient information.


  For questions about a Labcorp invoice, patients can contact Labcorp Patient Billing.
  The billing office can review the invoice and insurance information. Payment channels
  are separate from questions about whether an insurance claim was filed or processed.


  This page describes Labcorp''s ordinary billing and insurance process. It does not
  state what a Medicare beneficiary should do after a supplier has expressly refused
  to file a Medicare claim.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Billing & Insurance Information | Labcorp
url: https://www.labcorp.com/patients/billing

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Filing a Claim | Medicare

  - url: https://www.medicare.gov/providers-services/claims-appeals-complaints/claims

  - source: Medicare

  - domain: medicare.gov


  ## Content


  # Filing a Claim


  Medicare explains that beneficiaries should need to file their own claims only in
  rare cases.


  If a claim has not been filed, the ordinary first step is to contact the doctor,
  provider, or supplier and ask that organization to file it. If the beneficiary has
  already contacted the provider or supplier and the claim still has not been filed,
  Medicare directs the beneficiary to check the filing time limit and explains that
  the beneficiary may need to file the claim.


  The patient-filed process uses the Patient''s Request for Medical Payment form,
  CMS-1490S, together with the required bill and supporting information. Filing a
  claim requests Medicare payment for a service or supply; it is not the same as filing
  an appeal against an existing Medicare decision.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Filing a Claim | Medicare
url: https://www.medicare.gov/providers-services/claims-appeals-complaints/claims

Based on your records, the bill is **from Labcorp—not the hospital**—for **$143.28**, and your insurance is **Original Medicare**. Labcorp has already told you it will not submit the invoice to Medicare, and Medicare has no claim or EOB on file.

### Recommended next step

1. **Contact Labcorp Patient Billing again** and ask them to:
   - Confirm in writing why they will not file the claim with Original Medicare.
   - Verify that your Medicare information and date of service are correct.
   - Explain whether the service was performed under circumstances where Medicare billing rules differ.
   - Place the account on hold while you clarify coverage, if possible.

2. **Then contact Medicare at 1-800-MEDICARE (1-800-633-4227)** and explain:
   > “Labcorp billed me for a laboratory test, has refused to submit the claim to Original Medicare, and there is no claim or EOB on file. What should I do, and am I permitted to submit CMS-1490S?”

   Medicare says the provider or supplier should normally file the claim first. If it still has not been filed after you contacted the supplier, Medicare may direct you to submit a **Patient’s Request for Medical Payment form (CMS-1490S)** with the bill and supporting documentation.

3. **Do not assume the balance is automatically your responsibility or pay it immediately** until Medicare confirms the correct process. Your bill is not due until **August 3, 2026**, but ask Labcorp whether the due date can be paused during the review.

You generally do **not** need to contact the hospital’s billing office unless the hospital issued a separate bill or needs to correct information sent to Labcorp.

Sources: [Labcorp billing and insurance information](https://www.labcorp.com/patients/billing) and [Medicare: Filing a Claim](https://www.medicare.gov/providers-services/claims-appeals-complaints/claims).

final_answer:
Based on your records, the bill is **from Labcorp—not the hospital**—for **$143.28**, and your insurance is **Original Medicare**. Labcorp has already told you it will not submit the invoice to Medicare, and Medicare has no claim or EOB on file.

### Recommended next step

1. **Contact Labcorp Patient Billing again** and ask them to:
   - Confirm in writing why they will not file the claim with Original Medicare.
   - Verify that your Medicare information and date of service are correct.
   - Explain whether the service was performed under circumstances where Medicare billing rules differ.
   - Place the account on hold while you clarify coverage, if possible.

2. **Then contact Medicare at 1-800-MEDICARE (1-800-633-4227)** and explain:
   > “Labcorp billed me for a laboratory test, has refused to submit the claim to Original Medicare, and there is no claim or EOB on file. What should I do, and am I permitted to submit CMS-1490S?”

   Medicare says the provider or supplier should normally file the claim first. If it still has not been filed after you contacted the supplier, Medicare may direct you to submit a **Patient’s Request for Medical Payment form (CMS-1490S)** with the bill and supporting documentation.

3. **Do not assume the balance is automatically your responsibility or pay it immediately** until Medicare confirms the correct process. Your bill is not due until **August 3, 2026**, but ask Labcorp whether the due date can be paused during the review.

You generally do **not** need to contact the hospital’s billing office unless the hospital issued a separate bill or needs to correct information sent to Labcorp.

Sources: [Labcorp billing and insurance information](https://www.labcorp.com/patients/billing) and [Medicare: Filing a Claim](https://www.medicare.gov/providers-services/claims-appeals-complaints/claims).

text.regex:
(?isx)
^
(?!
  .*\b(?:if|unless|only\s+if)\b
  [^.!?\n]{0,220}
  \b(?:still|again|continue|continues|fail|fails|failed|unresolved|doesn[’']?t|won[’']?t|wouldn[’']?t|refuses?)\b
  [^.!?\n]{0,220}
  (?:
    CMS[\s_-]*1490S
    |Patient[’']?s\s+Request\s+for\s+Medical\s+Payment
    |(?:file|submit|send|complete|use)[^.!?\n]{0,90}(?:claim|Medicare)
  )
)
(?!
  .*(?:\A|[.!?]\s+|\n\s*(?:[-*]\s*)?)
  (?:
    (?:contact|call|ask)\s+(?:Labcorp|the\s+hospital|hospital)[^.!?\n]{0,45}\bfirst\b
    |(?:the\s+)?(?:first|primary)\s+(?:step|contact|route|destination)[^.!?\n]{0,70}(?:Labcorp|the\s+hospital|hospital)
    |primary\s+contact\s*(?:is|:)?\s*(?:Labcorp|the\s+hospital|hospital)
    |recommended\s+next\s+step[^.!?\n]{0,70}(?:Labcorp|the\s+hospital|hospital)
  )
)
(?=
  .*?(?:
    (?:Labcorp|the\s+(?:lab(?:oratory)?|supplier|provider))
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:
      refus(?:e|ed|es|ing)
      |declin(?:e|ed|es|ing)
      |won[’']?t
      |can[’']?t
      |(?:will|would|did|does|has|had)\s+not
      |failed
    )
    [^.!?\n]{0,90}
    (?:file|submit|send|bill)
    [^.!?\n]{0,90}
    (?:claim|invoice|Medicare|it|this)
    |
    (?:claim|invoice)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:was\s+)?(?:not\s+(?:filed|submitted|sent)|refused|declined)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:by\s+)?(?:Labcorp|the\s+(?:lab(?:oratory)?|supplier|provider))
  )
)
(?=
  .*?(?:
    (?:you|the\s+patient|the\s+beneficiary|patient|beneficiary)
    [^.!?\n]{0,35}
    (?:should|must|need(?:s)?\s+to|may\s+need\s+to|can\s+now|may\s+now)
    [^.!?\n]{0,35}
    (?:file|submit|send|complete|use)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:
      CMS[\s_-]*1490S
      |Patient[’']?s\s+Request\s+for\s+Medical\s+Payment
      |(?:patient|beneficiary|self)[\s_-]*(?:filed|submitted)\s+(?:Medicare\s+)?claim
      |(?:your|their|the\s+patient[’']?s|the\s+beneficiary[’']?s|own)\s+(?:Medicare\s+)?claim
      |claim[^.!?\n]{0,30}(?:with|to)\s+Medicare
    )
    |
    (?:the\s+)?(?:next|current|recommended|primary)\s+(?:step|route|action|destination)
    [^.!?\n]{0,55}
    (?:file|submit|send|complete|use)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:
      CMS[\s_-]*1490S
      |Patient[’']?s\s+Request\s+for\s+Medical\s+Payment
      |(?:patient|beneficiary|self)[\s_-]*(?:filed|submitted)\s+(?:Medicare\s+)?claim
      |(?:your|their|own)\s+(?:Medicare\s+)?claim
      |claim[^.!?\n]{0,30}(?:with|to)\s+Medicare
    )
    |
    (?:now|therefore|at\s+this\s+point|for\s+that\s+reason)
    [^.!?\n]{0,45}
    (?:file|submit|send|complete|use)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:
      CMS[\s_-]*1490S
      |Patient[’']?s\s+Request\s+for\s+Medical\s+Payment
      |(?:patient|beneficiary|self)[\s_-]*(?:filed|submitted)\s+(?:Medicare\s+)?claim
      |(?:your|their|own)\s+(?:Medicare\s+)?claim
      |claim[^.!?\n]{0,30}(?:with|to)\s+Medicare
    )
    |
    (?:\A|[.!?;]\s+|\n\s*(?:[-*]\s*)?)
    (?:file|submit|send|complete|use)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:
      CMS[\s_-]*1490S
      |Patient[’']?s\s+Request\s+for\s+Medical\s+Payment
      |(?:patient|beneficiary|self)[\s_-]*(?:filed|submitted)\s+(?:Medicare\s+)?claim
      |(?:your|their|own)\s+(?:Medicare\s+)?claim
      |claim[^.!?\n]{0,30}(?:with|to)\s+Medicare
    )
    |
    (?:you|the\s+patient|the\s+beneficiary)
    [^.!?\n]{0,35}
    (?:should|must|need(?:s)?\s+to|may\s+need\s+to)
    [^.!?\n]{0,25}
    (?:contact|call)\s+Medicare
    [^.!?\n]{0,80}
    (?:to|for\s+help\s+(?:to|with))
    [^.!?\n]{0,35}
    (?:file|submit|complete|use)
    [^.!?\n]{0,80}
    (?:claim|CMS[\s_-]*1490S|Patient[’']?s\s+Request\s+for\s+Medical\s+Payment)
  )
)
.*
$
